name: Mundus Imaginalis
definition: The mundus imaginalis (Latin for 'imaginal world') is an intermediate ontological realm between the physical world and the world of pure intellect. Coined by Henry Corbin to translate the Arabic 'alam al-mithal, it designates a real order of reality perceived by the imaginative faculty, not to be confused with the imaginary or fantasy.
wikipedia: Henry Corbin was a French philosopher, theologian, and Iranologist, professor of Islamic studies at the École pratique des hautes études. He was influential in extending the modern study of traditional Islamic philosophy from early falsafa to later and "mystical" figures such as Suhrawardi, Ibn Arabi, and Mulla Sadra Shirazi. With works such as Histoire de la philosophie islamique (1964), he challenged the common European view that philosophy in the Islamic world declined after Averroes and Avic
